summary: Kenneth Suna provides educational content on evaluating dividend safety for Starwood Property Trust (STWD), a real estate investment trust with a 9.74% dividend yield. The video teaches how to research dividend consistency using tools like Seeking Alpha and Stock Target Advisor, but contains no options trading strategies or rules applicable to Brian Norton's Premium Desk covered call and cash-secured put wheel strategy.
takeaways: Dividend payout ratio of 93% is considered high and warrants scrutiny
Starwood Property Trust pays quarterly dividends (not monthly like some REITs)
Check 10-year dividend history for consistency; STWD has maintained 48 cents/quarter unchanged for 10 years despite stock price volatility
Use Seeking Alpha dividend metrics (safety grade, consistency, 5-year growth, payout ratio) to evaluate dividend stocks

Cross-reference Seeking Alpha with Stock Target Advisor to get pros (low debt, positive cash flow, high market cap, high dividend return) and cons (poor returns, overpriced, high volatility, low dividend growth)
Over 5 years STWD is down 15%; over 10 years down 19%, but consistent dividend may present buying opportunity during downturns
title: Is the Dividend Safe?
